hazards.source: City of Cape Town Climate Change Strategy, 2021; IPCC AR6
hazards.description: Cape Town faces recurring and intensifying periods of water scarcity. The 2015-2018 drought nearly led to Day Zero.
hazards.impact: Severe water rationing, economic losses, public health risks, and social unrest.

hazards.otherHazardDetails: Combined effect of sea-level rise and increased storm surge intensity.
hazards.impact: Damage to property and infrastructure, displacement of communities, and salinization of coastal aquifers.

goals.description: Ensure a safe, reliable, and resilient drinking water supply for all residents by diversifying water sources to include at least 300 million litres per day from non-surface water sources, such as desalination, groundwater, and water reuse, reducing reliance on rain-fed dams.

actions.title: Water Demand Management and Conservation Program

status.otherStatusDetails: Program includes tiered water tariffs, leak detection and repair, and public awareness campaigns.

actions.title: Urban Greening Initiative for Heat Island Mitigation

status.otherStatusDetails: Focus on planting indigenous, drought-tolerant trees in priority neighborhoods.

projects.title: Cape Flats Aquifer Recharge and Abstraction Project

cases.question: What is the city's goal regarding drinking water?
Which hazards are associated with water supply disruption?
